heading: Official certificates or writings
label: 1018 Official certificates or writings
text: Whoever, being a public officer or other person authorized by any law of the United States to make or give a certificate or other writing, knowingly makes and delivers as true such a certificate or writing, containing any statement which he knows to be false, in a case where the punishment thereof is not elsewhere expressly provided by law, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 195 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 10635 Stat. 1107Minor changes were made in phraseology.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $500”.Pub. L. 103–322
